does: This paper articulates an organizational stance on AI policy and political engagement, laying out core principles that prioritize transparency about the company's positions, support for regulatory frameworks that promote safe AI development, investment in AI safety research, and clear boundaries around unauthorized political representation. The work establishes that the organization operates with deliberate separation between its technical and safety work and external political advocacy, rejecting the notion that any single outside group can claim to represent the company's interests in policy discussions.
connects: This statement builds on the emerging discourse around corporate responsibility in AI development that has grown as the field has matured from academic research into commercial deployment. It responds to broader conversations about industry self-regulation versus government oversight, drawing on earlier discussions in the AI ethics and safety community about the need for meaningful stakeholder engagement in policy formation. The emphasis on transparency connects to principles articulated across technology policy debates regarding corporate accountability and the tension between business interests and public welfare.
enables: This framework establishes a foundation for more structured dialogue between AI developers and policymakers by clarifying where a major AI company stands on key regulatory issues. It creates space for constructive engagement with government bodies, safety researchers, and civil society without the confusion of competing external claims. By explicitly centering AI safety as a policy priority alongside broader governance questions, this position potentially enables more informed regulation that accounts for technical realities while maintaining clear separation between technical decisions and political representation.
